title: Project profile (project-profile/v1)
description: The schema-validated YAML report kitsoki's dev-story init phase emits: how to instantiate dev-story for THIS project. The declarative source the generated .kitsoki/stories/<id>-dev/ instance compiles from. See docs/proposals/project-init.md.

id.description: Kebab-case project id; seeds the generated instance dir .kitsoki/stories/<id>-dev/.

generated.description: Provenance. Stamped by the deterministic apply script (scripts cannot read the clock).

by.description: kitsoki version / agent agent that synthesized it.

mining_job.description: session-mining job id this profile was fine-tuned from (links the `mining` block).

goals.description: Named story or lifecycle-phase goals. Each goal states measurable postconditions and points them at setup_plan.verifications by id.

root.description: Absolute or repo-relative checkout root. '.' for the current repo.

branch_pattern.description: Normal working-branch template, for example feature/{issue_id}-{slug}.

branch_issue_id.description: Whether normal working branches include a ticket or issue id.

dev_server.description: How to bring the project up locally and know it is ready. Omit for non-server projects (cli/library).

ready.description: Deterministic readiness probe — the boot→probe→teardown verification init_verify runs (one-shot; not a persistent server).

target.description: URL (http), host:port (tcp), regex (log), or shell command (command).

environments.description: local / dev / staging / prod (and custom). Where each runs and how it is configured/deployed.

config_ref.description: Path/ref to the env's config (.env, k8s manifest, terraform dir, …).

gated.description: Whether deploying here requires explicit operator confirmation (external/destructive).

commands.description: The canonical command for each common dev verb. The integration seam: init reuses these rather than inventing its own. Projects may add named workflow commands.

mechanisms.description: Existing test mechanisms init integrates with rather than replaces.

output_workflows.description: Project-specific named output/review workflows such as web-player, html bundle, pdf, or video export.

golden_path.description: The simplest end-to-end scenario init proves works (UI projects: a clickthrough; api/cli: a smoke).

spec.description: Path to a spec/flow fixture that drives the golden path deterministically.

conventions.description: Working-dir conventions. `source` records whether init recommends kitsoki's (.context/.artifacts/.worktrees), keeps the project's own, or a hybrid.

manage.description: Whether init appends a managed block to .gitignore on confirm.

tracker.description: Issue or work tracker metadata discovered for this project. tracker.sources is the canonical ordered set; provider/project/repo remain readable for legacy profiles.

provider.description: Legacy single-provider metadata. New profiles should declare tracker.sources.

project.description: Legacy single-project locator. New profiles should declare each source's args.

sources.description: Ordered, composable ticket sources exposed through host.ticket_federation. Source ids are stable user-facing identities, independent of provider-local ticket numbers.

id.description: Stable source id used in federated refs such as upstream:123.

label.description: Unambiguous label shown in ticket lists.

provider.description: Concrete statically registered ticket host id, for example host.local_files.ticket or host.gh.ticket. Raw .star paths are direct bindings and cannot be composed as federation sources.

kind.description: Presentation/provider class such as local, github, jira, or a future provider kind.

mode.description: Whether the source is local to the checkout or backed by a remote service.

args.description: Provider-specific arguments passed directly to the concrete ticket host.

locator_keys.description: Optional provider-specific args that identify this source and must override ambient runtime values, for example project or queue. repo, root, and workdir are implicit when present in args.

pull_requests.description: Where pull requests are opened and which project policy they follow.

provider.description: Pull-request provider, for example github, gitlab, bitbucket, local, or none.

repository.description: Repository slug or URL where pull requests are opened.

template.description: Repo-relative pull-request template path, or an empty string when the project has no template.

rules.description: Project rules the dev-story instance should honor — discovered from rules files, mined from sessions, or stated by the operator.

ref.description: Path#anchor to the authoritative statement, when the rule lives in a file.

kitsoki.description: How to instantiate dev-story for this project — the generalized, discovered form of .kitsoki/stories/kitsoki-dev/ and stories/gears-rust/.

story.description: The root kit story the instance imports (dev-story by default; any installed kit that declares a kit.yaml root: block naming it also qualifies -- see D6 root generalization, .context/kits-implementation-plan.md).

story_pack.description: Named first-run story pack selected during onboarding. This records the install-like starter group; it is not a runtime fence.

enabled_stories.description: Adoption-scope list for teams starting with a focused subset of the dev-story catalog. This records what to emphasize first; it is not a runtime fence.

path.description: Where the generated instance app.yaml is written, e.g. .kitsoki/stories/<id>-dev/app.yaml.

bindings.description: Concrete host bindings for the root story's declared host_interfaces (dev-story's default set: ticket/vcs/ci/workspace/transport -- see kitsoki.story). Each value is a host id, e.g. host.git or host.local_files.ticket, or a .star script path resolved from the project root. Which keys are actually required is root-kit-dependent and checked semantically (internal/projectprofile.validateSemantic), not fixed here, since a non-default root kit may declare different interface names.

external_profile.description: dev-story's External-target profile world keys, when retargeting a foreign repo (see stories/gears-rust/). Omit for an in-repo instance.

harness_profile.description: Named .kitsoki.yaml harness profile this instance defaults to.

dev_story_profile.description: Reusable dev-story convention knobs. Community/base stories define the keys; org/project profiles choose values.

docs.description: PRD/design document placement, template, and follow-up ticket policy.

bugfix.description: Project toolchain gates projected into the bugfix import.

mining.description: The session-mining evidence the profile was fine-tuned from (recency sample over the project's transcripts). Distinct from stories/dev-story-mining/, which enriches dev-story's own gates; here it tunes THIS project's profile.

sources.description: Associated transcript sources found during deterministic discovery. Evidence only; mining still requires explicit consent/config.

onboarding.description: Reusable onboarding anchors: which starter story was selected, what repo evidence drove adaptation, and the replay-test commits/fixtures.

base_story.description: Reusable starter story selected during onboarding, e.g. dev-story.

base_story_title.description: Human title for the selected starter story.

base_story_reason.description: Why this starter story was selected for the repo.

story_pack.description: Named starter pack chosen in the first-run menu.

story_pack_title.description: Human title for the selected starter pack.

story_pack_summary.description: Short explanation of the selected starter pack.

starter_stories.description: Focused story set the team should start with before expanding to more of dev-story.

expansion_policy.description: How to expand from the focused starter story set.

resolutions.description: Per-field onboarding provenance. The stored value is the last resolved value, allowing reruns to distinguish a managed value from an operator edit.

field.description: Dotted path to the canonical project-profile field.

value.description: The last value onboarding resolved for this field.

evidence.description: Repo evidence or operator decision supporting the value.

update.description: Exact file and field location the operator can update.

notice.description: User-facing explanation shown when onboarding had to use a default.

repo_patterns.description: Deterministic repo evidence used to adapt the starter story.

story_customizations.description: Starter-story adaptations applied or proposed for this project-local instance.

baseline_commit.description: Git commit before Kitsoki onboarding files were introduced.

first_onboarding_commit.description: First commit produced by onboarding, useful for diff oracles.

deterministic_flow.description: Flow fixture used to replay onboarding without a real LLM.

recording_policy.description: Whether this profile is approved for explicit, operator-triggered live recording runs.

setup_plan.description: The propose-then-confirm action list. Nothing here is executed until the operator confirms at init_review.

writes.description: Files init will create/merge on confirm (the generated instance, .kitsoki.yaml additions, this profile, …).

verifications.description: The readiness checks init_verify runs on confirm. `required` gates failures into init_done's report; `advisory` is best-effort.

fields.description: Canonical project-profile fields inspected by a native or metadata verification.

readiness.description: Verification results, filled by init_verify. A dry-run report leaves status: not-run.

statement.description: The outcome this story or phase exists to establish.

requires.description: Other named goals that must be satisfied first.

verification.description: Id of the setup_plan.verifications entry that measures this postcondition.

applicable.description: False records an explicit, reviewable not-applicable outcome instead of inventing a check.

use.description: kitsoki = adopt kitsoki's default dir; existing = the project already has one here; custom = operator-chosen path; none = decline this convention.

media.description: Regenerable media descriptor, identical to lifecycle-taxonomy's (docs/proposals/lifecycle-taxonomy.md).

produced_by.description: Deterministic producer (Playwright spec, flow fixture) so the evidence is regenerable.
